title: 📊 Engineering Overview [v3]
description: Welcome page — dashboard index, architecture overview, schema drift status, and data sync controls.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE('Live: ' || (SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key = 'org') || '/' || (SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key = 'repo'), 'Not yet synced') AS "Data Source"

options.content:
## 📋 Dashboard Index

| # | Dashboard | Purpose | Tags |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [🚀 Deployment Frequency](/d/edu-deployment-frequency) | How often code ships to production | DORA, Throughput |
| 2 | [⏱️ Lead Time for Changes](/d/edu-lead-time) | Time from first commit to production deployment | DORA, Throughput |
| 3 | [🔥 Change Failure Rate](/d/edu-change-failure-rate) | % of deployments that cause an incident | DORA, Stability |
| 4 | [🏥 Mean Time to Recovery](/d/edu-mean-time-to-recovery) | Time to resolve an incident after it opens | DORA, Stability |
| 5 | [🤖 Copilot Adoption](/d/edu-copilot-adoption) | Active seats, acceptance rate, usage by editor | Copilot |
| 6 | [💻 Copilot Code Impact](/d/edu-copilot-code-impact) | Lines suggested vs accepted, LoC trends | Copilot |
| 7 | [🔬 DORA × Copilot](/d/edu-dora-vs-copilot) | Deployment freq & lead time split by Copilot users vs non-users | DORA, Copilot |
| 8 | [👤 Per-User Copilot Metrics](/d/edu-per-user-copilot) | Acceptance rate and activity per developer | Copilot |

---

## 🏗️ Why ELT — not ETL

Most production dashboards use **ETL** (Extract → **Transform** → Load): data is reshaped by application code before it ever reaches the database. That keeps queries simple, but it hides what the underlying API actually returns — intermediate column names, flattened structures, and computed fields make it hard to understand what you're really measuring or to adapt the pipeline to a new org.

This tool is built for **learning**, not production hardening. It uses **ELT** (Extract → Load → **Transform**):

| Step | Where it happens | What it means |
|---|---|---|
| **Extract** | `src/github/*.ts` | Fetch raw JSON from the GitHub API via Octokit |
| **Load** | PostgreSQL | Store fields verbatim — column names match the API field names exactly |
| **Transform** | Grafana SQL panels | All aggregations, joins, and calculations live here, in plain SQL |

Because the schema mirrors the API, you can read a dashboard SQL panel and trace every field back to the GitHub documentation. There are no hidden mappings or invented column names.

```
GitHub API  →  Octokit SDK (TypeScript)  →  PostgreSQL  →  Grafana SQL
               src/github/*.ts               db/schema.sql   dashboards/*.json
```

**Auto-migration** — when GitHub adds a new field, the next sync detects it and runs `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` automatically. The _Schema Drift_ section below tracks what has been added.

**Data refresh** — click **Refresh Data Now** to pull the latest 28-day rolling window from GitHub.

## ⚙️ Environment

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | Classic PAT — needs `repo`, `read:org`, `admin:org` scopes |
| `GITHUB_ENTERPRISE` | Enterprise slug (e.g. `Octodemo`) |
| `GITHUB_ORG` | Organisation name |
| `GITHUB_REPO` | Repository name |

Why is a sync needed? Data is pulled from the GitHub API in a rolling 28-day window and stored locally in PostgreSQL. It does not update automatically. Click Refresh Data Now to fetch the latest PRs, deployments, workflow runs, issues, and Copilot metrics (~15–30 s). The sync history table below shows recent jobs, their status, and how many records were written.

targets.rawSql: SELECT UPPER(status) AS "Last Sync Status" FROM sync_jobs ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 1
SELECT COALESCE((SELECT SUM(v::int) FROM jsonb_each_text(records_synced) x(k,v)), 0)::bigint AS "Records Synced" FROM sync_jobs ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content: Calls POST /sync · sync server on localhost:3005

targets.rawSql: SELECT TO_CHAR(started_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI') || ' UTC' AS "Last Sync" FROM sync_jobs ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 1

SELECT id AS "Job", status AS "Status", TO_CHAR(started_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI') AS "Started", COALESCE((SELECT SUM(v::int) FROM jsonb_each_text(records_synced) x(k,v)), 0)::bigint AS "Records" FROM sync_jobs ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 10

options.content:
Why does schema drift happen? The GitHub API occasionally adds new fields to its responses. On each sync, this app compares incoming API fields against the current database columns. If a new field is found, it automatically runs ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN — the live database is updated immediately, no restart needed.

Live DB vs schema.sql The automatic migration keeps the live database current, but it does not update the checked-in v3/src/db/schema.sql file. The counter below shows how many columns have been auto-added to the live database but are still missing from that file. Until schema.sql is updated, a fresh database rebuild will be missing those columns.

How to update schema.sql

  1. Check the Drift History table below to identify which tables and fields were auto-added.
  2. Open v3/src/db/schema.sql and add each missing column to the matching CREATE TABLE block using the type shown in the drift log.
  3. Commit: git add v3/src/db/schema.sql && git commit -m "chore: sync schema.sql with auto-migrated columns"
  4. No database rebuild needed — the columns already exist in the live DB.

targets.rawSql:
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT schema_drift
  FROM sync_jobs
  WHERE status = 'success'
  ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1
),
all_drift AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    (entry->>'table') || '.' || (col->>'field') AS col_key
  FROM latest,
    jsonb_array_elements(latest.schema_drift) AS entry,
    jsonb_array_elements(entry->'auto_applied') AS col
  WHERE latest.schema_drift IS NOT NULL AND latest.schema_drift != '[]'::jsonb
)
SELECT COALESCE(COUNT(*)::int, 0) AS "Columns not in schema.sql" FROM all_drift

-- One row per drifted table per sync job; 'None' for clean syncs
SELECT
  j.id                                                   AS "Job",
  TO_CHAR(j.started_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC',
          'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI')                          AS "Synced At",
  entry->>'table'                                        AS "Table",
  (
    SELECT string_agg(col->>'field', ', '
           ORDER BY col->>'field')
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(entry->'auto_applied') col
  )                                                      AS "Columns Added"
FROM sync_jobs j,
     jsonb_array_elements(j.schema_drift) entry
WHERE j.schema_drift IS NOT NULL
  AND j.schema_drift <> '[]'::jsonb
  AND jsonb_array_length(
        COALESCE(entry->'auto_applied', '[]'::jsonb)) > 0

UNION ALL

SELECT
  j.id                                                   AS "Job",
  TO_CHAR(j.started_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC',
          'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI')                          AS "Synced At",
  '(none)'                                               AS "Table",
  'None'                                                 AS "Columns Added"
FROM sync_jobs j
WHERE j.schema_drift IS NULL
   OR j.schema_drift = '[]'::jsonb
   OR NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM jsonb_array_elements(j.schema_drift) e
        WHERE jsonb_array_length(
                COALESCE(e->'auto_applied', '[]'::jsonb)) > 0
      )

ORDER BY 1 DESC, 3
LIMIT 30
